message:
feat(agent): safety barriers for side-effect tool execution

Refactor execute_tool_calls() to distinguish read-only tools from
side-effect tools, preventing race conditions from concurrent edits:

- Read-only tools (read, grep, find, ls) accumulate in pending_parallel
  and execute via buffer_unordered(MAX_CONCURRENT_TOOLS)
- Side-effect tools (edit, write, bash, etc.) trigger a flush barrier:
  all pending parallel tools complete first, then the side-effect tool
  executes sequentially — prevents concurrent file edits or conflicting
  bash commands
- Tool outputs stored in indexed Vec<Option<...>> so results stay
  ordered regardless of execution order
- Abort handling integrated into both parallel flushes and sequential
  execution with early break on AbortSignal
- Steering (queued user message) still discards subsequent tool results
  from conversation history via skip_tool_call() helper, even if tools
  already executed in a parallel batch
- Revert on_event parameter from AgentEventHandler alias back to
  &Arc<dyn Fn> for borrow-checker compatibility with the new closure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
